title: OpenAI / Azure / ElevenLabs provider production readiness and cost guard
repoConfirmed: Strict production provider policy requires ElevenLabs voice, OpenAI transcription, Azure pronunciation, and OpenAI Script Studio.
Production preflight, voice preflight, and pronunciation preflight scripts exist.
Cost kill switches exist for OpenAI, Azure, ElevenLabs, and Storage uploads.
Upload routes check the Storage upload kill switch before storing new audio.
OpenAI transcription, Azure pronunciation, and ElevenLabs voice provider paths use server-side boundaries.
Generated voice audio is normalized into app-owned replay.
Quota events are non-blocking operational metadata, not hard enforcement.
ElevenLabs provider cleanup boundary exists behind destructive guard, but normal checks do not execute real delete.
humanConfirmationRequired: OpenAI project, billing, usage limit, alert owner, and model availability.
Azure Speech resource, region, quota, billing, and pronunciation assessment availability.
ElevenLabs plan, credits, voice cloning availability, API availability, and deletion capability.
Provider dashboard usage, quota, and budget alert visibility.
Production env presence only for required provider keys and model settings.
Kill switch operation proof for OpenAI, Azure, ElevenLabs, and Storage uploads.
Support escalation owner and provider outage communication path.
Provider cleanup and retention behavior for account deletion and Brush-up revoke.

storeBlockers: Refresh dashboard/billing/alert proof for the exact release environment.
Refresh mobile/WebView recording and PCM normalization smoke.
Prove provider cleanup/deletion with disposable account flow.
Confirm Brush-up create/delete/retention/cost behavior before Gate 3.5 implementation.

conditions: Provider clone/material creation exceeds Vercel Function timeout.
Provider retry/backoff must continue after user leaves the page.
Cleanup/revoke must be guaranteed without operator action.
Audio conversion becomes CPU-heavy or large-file-heavy.
Concurrent Brush-up requests need durable locking beyond DB constraints.
